frame_analyses.description: medyoucate logo and title card: 'Two-handed knot tying' by Dr. Ana Gabersek, University Hospital Salzburg
Close-up of Penrose drain with red suture and marked points visible on suture block. Two-handed knot tying technique demonstration begins.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup shows clear mark points and red Penrose drain ready for suturing

frame_analyses.description: Left hand positioning suture with needle preparation visible. Right hand holding suture end. Mark points clearly visible on drain block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates initial hand positioning and suture control setup

frame_analyses.description: Both hands working suture, demonstrating correct needle approach angle to the marked entry point on drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean perpendicular approach to entry mark; good hand coordination

frame_analyses.description: Suture being threaded through marked points. Both entry and exit points visible; suture passes cleanly through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper two-handed control during suture passage

frame_analyses.description: Right hand leading first throw (surgeon's knot/double throw). Suture wrapped around left hand instrument with controlled tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear demonstration of double throw wrap; right hand leading

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with clean cinching motion. Knot tightens against drain with appropriate tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth knot formation; appropriate tension to secure without over-tightening

frame_analyses.description: Hand switch: left hand now leads second throw (single throw). Demonstrates alternating hand technique clearly.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand exchange between throws; left hand takes leading role

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completing with left hand leading. Knot cinches smoothly; drain remains secure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper single throw after surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Hand switch back to right hand leading third throw (single throw). Maintains consistent technique across all three throws.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean hand alternation; right hand resumes lead position

frame_analyses.description: Third throw completes with right hand leading. Final knot positioned at mark line on drain. Knot appears secure and properly tensioned.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot well-positioned; demonstrates stability of complete knot sequence

frame_analyses.description: Finished knot shown on drain. Suture ends visible and drain slit closed properly. Video transitions to completion card.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows clean knot placement and secure closure

confidence_rationale: This is an educational/instructional video produced by medyoucate featuring Dr. Ana Gabersek demonstrating two-handed knot tying technique on a suture training block. The video contains title cards, narration context, and staged demonstration—not an actual FLS task performance under timed conditions. Per classification rules, instructional content is NOT scored; teaching content is extracted instead.
cannot_determine: Actual mark placement deviation (demonstration uses pre-marked block, not an evaluable trainee attempt)
technique_summary: Expert-level demonstration of intracorporeal knot tying technique with clear hand alternation, proper knot sequence (surgeon's knot followed by two single throws), and smooth instrument control. Designed as educational content for trainee instruction.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper two-handed suture control and positioning
Perpendicular needle entry angle to avoid superficial bites
Surgeon's knot (double throw) as first throw for secure foundation
Hand alternation protocol: right leads throw 1, left leads throw 2, right leads throw 3
Smooth cinching motion under controlled tension
Knot placement at marked closure site
Clean final knot appearance with secure drain slit closure
verbal_cues: Title identifies this as 'Two-handed knot tying' instruction by Dr. Ana Gabersek, University Hospital Salzburg
common_errors_addressed: Avoids excessive tension that could damage drain
Demonstrates proper hand switching between throws to ensure knot security
Shows perpendicular suture passage to maintain drain integrity
Demonstrates clean knot formation without slack or gaps

note: Video is educational/instructional content featuring expert demonstration. Not scored as performance. Extracted for teaching value only.
